item.title: Microsoft’s AI Chief Says Anthropic Models Are Too Expensive
item.description: The software company’s AI chief is working to build cheaper in-house models

item.title: Ramp Notches $44 Billion Valuation in New Funding Round
item.description: Ramp, a corporate spending management platform, has raised $750 million in a new funding round at a $44 billion valuation, a sign of investor enthusiasm for using artificial intelligence to help companies with their bookkeeping.

item.title: Polymarket Lets You Bet on Vibes
item.description: By taking bets on social media clout, the prediction market has made itself vulnerable to bots.

item.title: Logistics Costs in US Surge to a Four-Year High
item.description: It’s a challenge for the Fed to combat inflation fueled by supply shocks

item.title: US Youth Job Prospects May Not Be as Bad as They Seem
item.description: The optimist’s take on the outlook for young workers in the age of AI.

item.title: SpaceX Promises a Bonanza for Wall Street’s Bankers
item.description: Bankers go all-out to win roles, make IPO a success

item.title: Business Warns Johannesburg Crisis Risks South African Recovery
item.description: South African business leaders warned that Johannesburg’s deepening fiscal and governance crisis risks undermining the country’s improving economic outlook, calling for urgent action to rebuild the nation’s commercial capital.

item.title: Ares’ Jacobson Slams ‘Disconnect’ Over Private Credit Headlines
item.description: Ares Management Corporation’s co-president Blair Jacobson said there’s a “real disconnect” between negative headlines about private credit and what the firm is seeing across its portfolio companies.

item.title: China’s Robotaxi Dilemma Shows AI Policy Tension Between Growth and Jobs
item.description: China leads the world on robotaxis, but is finding that competitive edge comes with a price.

item.title: Irish GDP Falls 12% in First Quarter as Multinationals Contract
item.description: Irish gross domestic product fell 12% in the first three months of the year as the multinational-dominated sector contracted.

item.title: Indonesia’s Radical Export Plan Takes Effect as Questions Swirl
item.description: Lingering uncertainty around Indonesia’s commodity export rules means traders are holding up some shipments just as radical changes come into effect, adding to strains on Southeast Asia’s largest economy.

item.title: India Considers Spending Curbs to Protect Fiscal Deficit Target
item.description: India’s government is weighing spending cuts across parts of the budget as higher oil prices inflate subsidy bills and threaten to derail its fiscal consolidation plans, according to officials familiar with the matter.

item.title: Arm’s $218 Billion Leap Makes It One of Market’s Priciest Stocks
item.description: Arm Holdings Plc has been a relatively expensive stock for investors to own since the company first issued American depositary receipts in 2023. But a wild surge that has nearly doubled the share price in a matter of weeks is pushing the valuation to levels investors rarely see.

item.title: EU Delays Bank Trading-Book Rules to Blunt Wall Street Advantage
item.description: The European Union’s executive arm has unveiled what it hopes will be its final workaround to shield its lenders from being temporarily hit by harsher trading-book rules than those faced by Wall Street rivals.

item.title: Trafigura Pays Record Dividend as Profit Soars Over $4 Billion
item.description: Trafigura Group made a record payout to its top traders and executives as the commodity trading giant reaped profits of over $4 billion in the first half of its financial year.

item.title: Banks Offload $1 Trillion Loan Risk to SRT Investors, IACPM Says
item.description: Banks had offloaded credit risk tied to more than €905 billion ($1 trillion) in loans as of the end of last year, up 26% from a year earlier, via the rapidly-growing market for significant risk transfers.

item.title: Thailand’s Richest Man Plans $4.3 Billion Expansion Amid AI Boom
item.description: Thailand’s richest man, Sarath Ratanavadi, plans to spend as much as 140 billion baht ($4.3 billion) through Gulf Development Pcl over the next five years to expand data centers and other infrastructure needed to support the artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: US Tech Sector Announces Most Job Cuts in Nearly Two Years
item.description: US technology companies in May announced the most job cuts in nearly two years as they ramp up spending on artificial intelligence.

item.title: Congo Eurobonds Defy Ebola as Investors Chase Rare Yield, AI Exposure
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo’s Eurobonds have remained resilient even as it grapples with an Ebola outbreak, buoyed by high yields and the nation’s exposure to metals that underpin the global artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: Reliance Traders Said to Game Plan in Case RBI Raises Rates
item.description: Traders in Reliance Industries Ltd.’s treasury department are strategizing over where to park the company’s cash in case the Reserve Bank of India starts raising interest rates in the coming months.

item.title: Fuji Media’s Real Estate Unit Attracts 1 Trillion Yen Bids
item.description: Fuji Media Holdings Inc. has received stronger-than-expected interest in its real estate subsidiary, attracting bidders including KKR & Co., Blackstone Inc. and Goldman Sachs Group Inc., people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: AI Boom Propels China Optical Maker to Top Weighting on CSI 300
item.description: A Chinese optical transceiver maker has overtaken a battery giant as the top-weighted firm in China’s equities benchmark, the latest sign of how a broadening AI rally is reshaping the world’s second-largest stock market.

item.title: SoftBank’s PayPay to Buy T&D’s Life Insurer for $840 Million
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp.’s payments unit is buying the life insurance unit of T&D Holdings Inc. for ¥134.3 billion ($840 million) to broaden its offerings and better compete in Japan’s ballooning fintech market.

item.title: BofA Says Foreign Buyers Lured by UK Yields Despite Politics
item.description: Bank of America predicts a streak of gilt purchases by foreign buyers extended through May, as some of the highest government yields in major developed nations offset political turbulence and inflation fears.

item.title: Foreigners Offload Japanese Stocks as AI Bubble Worries Grow
item.description: Overseas investors turned net sellers of Japanese shares for the first time in two months last week, ending a buying streak that helped drive the nation’s benchmark to multiple all-time highs.

item.title: Japan Officials Met Metals, LNG Firms in Unusual Moscow Talks
item.description: Japanese officials who visited Moscow last week met commodity exporters along with Russian government representatives in a rare high-level commercial contact between their countries since President Vladimir Putin ordered the 2022 full scale invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon on Running a Bank in the Age of AI
item.description: Why he doesn't see an AI jobs apocalypse coming to Wall Street.

item.title: Odd Lots: Goldman’s Solomon on Banks in the Age of AI (Podcast)
item.description: There’s a lot of debate about the future of AI — not just whether it will produce the returns investors are expecting, but also if AI will lead to mass worker displacement. Big banks are the perfect prism through which to explore some of these questions. Not only are they deploying AI very quickly, but they have a wide range of workers who are using the technology, from back-office employees to junior analysts to the most senior investment bankers. In this episode, we speak with David Solomon, c

item.title: Goldman Erects Lobby Rockets as Morgan Stanley IPO Rivalry Heats Up
item.description: No sooner had Goldman Sachs Group Inc. locked in top billing for the SpaceX IPO than Morgan Stanley dashed off a note to clients that it was actually co-lead on the deal, noting that their listing is in alphabetical order.

item.title: Ultra-Rich Africans Piling into Property to Preserve Wealth, Standard Bank Says
item.description: Africans with at least $50 million in investable assets are increasingly buying property for wealth preservation, diversification, and potential rental income, according to the continent’s biggest lender by assets.
